jokes.text: Oh my gosh, is this what marriage is? / Nope, this is just left over from the picnic yesterday
jokes.setup: David dramatically asks if domestic scene represents marriage
jokes.punchline: Patrick deflates the moment with mundane picnic leftovers explanation
jokes.explanation: Strong character-driven misdirection. David's dramatic interpretation vs Patrick's pragmatic reality. Clean structure with good economy.

jokes.text: Hmm, you could've lied
jokes.setup: Patrick gives honest but unromantic explanation
jokes.punchline: David's dry suggestion that Patrick should have been dishonest for romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect David line - values presentation over truth. Excellent economy and character voice.

jokes.text: I look like I crawled out from under a bridge
jokes.setup: Patrick compliments David's appearance in photos
jokes.punchline: David's vivid, dramatic self-criticism
jokes.explanation: Classic David self-deprecation with colorful imagery. Solid character voice but not particularly original.

jokes.text: David's elaborate engagement announcement fantasy
jokes.setup: Simple suggestion to tell a few people after the show
jokes.punchline: Increasingly detailed and self-aggrandizing fantasy involving tears, speeches, and himself as emotional centerpiece
jokes.explanation: Exceptional character comedy. Perfectly captures David's narcissism and need for drama. Great escalation from simple announcement to elaborate production.

jokes.text: Great to know you've barely given this any thought
jokes.setup: David's incredibly detailed fantasy
jokes.punchline: Patrick's dry observation that contradicts David's claim of not thinking about it
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response. Clean irony and great character dynamic. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Good luck, honey! / It's 'break a leg'
jokes.setup: Patrick gives casual well-wishes
jokes.punchline: David corrects him with theater terminology
jokes.explanation: Predictable correction joke but fits David's character. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: You expect me to bring this with me?! This frame is like, ten pounds!
jokes.setup: Johnny's sentimental gift for Alexis's travels
jokes.punchline: Reveal of impractically heavy picture frame
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy premise. Johnny's well-meaning but impractical gesture perfectly characterized.

jokes.text: 'Break all your legs' - A well-intentioned, if not slightly misguided sentiment from the Elmdale Arts Council
jokes.setup: Theater tradition of saying 'break a leg'
jokes.punchline: Small town council's literal interpretation for multiple performers
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay showing small-town misunderstanding of theater traditions. Moira's verbose delivery adds character flavor.

jokes.text: Well, that seems like poor planning
jokes.setup: Ted leaving tonight, Alexis leaving in a week
jokes.punchline: Moira's judgmental assessment of their romantic logistics
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - reducing romantic gesture to efficiency critique. Good character voice.

jokes.text: Moira offering Alexis the publicity job while she's leaving for Galapagos
jokes.setup: Moira builds up to big opportunity announcement
jokes.punchline: Complete disconnect with Alexis's travel plans she just mentioned
jokes.explanation: Good situational irony. Moira's self-absorption prevents her from connecting obvious dots.

jokes.text: Well, again, poor planning
jokes.setup: Alexis points out timeline conflict Moira created
jokes.punchline: Moira doubles down on blaming Alexis for poor planning
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Moira's inability to accept responsibility. Escalates the absurdity.

jokes.text: I'm going to support my boyfriend, and hopefully, eat, pray, love, a bit for myself
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining her travel motivations
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to pop culture self-discovery trope
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis line - mixing genuine emotion with pop culture reference. Good character voice.

jokes.text: and the energy is unusually light around here
jokes.setup: Johnny calling for family moment because David's away
jokes.punchline: Admission that David's presence creates heavy energy
jokes.explanation: Clever family dynamic observation. Johnny's accidental honesty about David's dramatic nature.

jokes.text: Moira Rose Enterprises, how may I direct your call?
jokes.setup: Phone rings during family moment
jokes.punchline: Moira answers personal phone as if she has a business empire
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira delusion of grandeur. Creates fake business empire from thin air.

jokes.text: Well, yes, my assistant is very busy
jokes.setup: Continuing fake business phone persona
jokes.punchline: Inventing non-existent assistant
jokes.explanation: Continues Moira's delusional business fantasy. Good escalation of the absurd persona.

jokes.text: Outside of criticizing someone's style choices
jokes.setup: David never uses word 'courage'
jokes.punchline: Except when judging fashion
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about David's priorities. Well-constructed callback to his fashion obsession.

jokes.text: There's something in the news about Oprah. She got new espadrilles
jokes.setup: David admits his happiness comes from Oprah news
jokes.punchline: Incredibly mundane 'news' about shoes
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor. Perfect David obsession with celebrity minutiae. Great misdirection from setup.

jokes.text: Patrick proposed, and I said yes
jokes.setup: Alexis pressures David for gossip
jokes.punchline: David's frustrated revelation of major news
jokes.explanation: Good payoff for mounting tension. David's exasperated delivery works well.

jokes.text: Alexis's overwhelming emotional reaction
jokes.setup: Happy news about David's engagement
jokes.punchline: Alexis becomes physically overwhelmed with emotion
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy showing Alexis's emotional nature. Decent execution but somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: It's just nerves. About the show
jokes.setup: David asks if she's okay after emotional outburst
jokes.punchline: Alexis deflects her emotional response about his leaving
jokes.explanation: Good emotional misdirection. Shows Alexis's difficulty expressing vulnerability about David's life changes.

jokes.text: Kind of looks like a vampire. A little skittish when approached
jokes.setup: Roland describing missing Stevie to authorities
jokes.punchline: His hilariously unflattering but accurate description
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures both Roland's tactlessness and Stevie's character. Memorable description that's both mean and accurate.

jokes.text: if you have a taser, you may want to use it
jokes.setup: Roland giving advice for finding Stevie
jokes.punchline: Suggesting extreme force for stage fright
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Roland's inappropriate advice. Shows his complete lack of social awareness.

jokes.text: I don't know how you're gonna feel about this, but I'm gonna need some hair samples
jokes.setup: David was last to see Stevie
jokes.punchline: Roland treating minor disappearance like serious crime requiring DNA evidence
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation. Roland's CSI delusions applied to community theater crisis.

jokes.text: Patrick proposed to me yesterday, and I may have told her about it
jokes.setup: Family pressures David for information about Stevie
jokes.punchline: David's reluctant admission reveals engagement news
jokes.explanation: Functional revelation that moves plot forward. David's reluctance adds character element.

jokes.text: She planned her brother-in-law's funeral for under a hundred bucks
jokes.setup: Suggesting Jocelyn could plan the wedding
jokes.punchline: Her budget planning credentials come from a funeral
jokes.explanation: Dark humor mixing celebration with death. Good character moment showing Roland's inappropriate comparisons.

jokes.text: When did this become about you?
jokes.setup: Moira making David's engagement about her show crisis
jokes.punchline: David pointing out the irony while being equally self-centered
jokes.explanation: Good irony showing David's lack of self-awareness. Both characters being equally self-centered.

jokes.text: What a unanimously disastrous day this is turning out to be!
jokes.setup: Multiple crises piling up
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic overstatement combining personal and professional disasters
jokes.explanation: Typical Moira dramatics. Good escalation but predictable character response.

jokes.text: What the fuck?!
jokes.setup: Chaos and engagement announcements
jokes.punchline: Stevie's blunt reaction to returning to mayhem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie reaction. Maximum economy and character voice, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: Or is it 'Misters?'
jokes.setup: Ray congratulating 'Mister and Mister'
jokes.punchline: Genuine confusion about proper terminology for same-sex marriage
jokes.explanation: Gentle comedy about social progress. Ray's well-meaning confusion is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I left my phone at home, which I realize was a really big mistake
jokes.setup: Stevie explains why she couldn't be reached during crisis
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement given the chaos her absence caused
jokes.explanation: Good understatement showing Stevie's dry humor about the situation she inadvertently created.

jokes.text: Moira in full costume pretending she was just dressed in theme to support
jokes.setup: Moira clearly prepared to replace Stevie
jokes.punchline: Her transparent lie about just being supportive
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy. Moira's obvious preparation contrasting with her casual claim.

jokes.text: I was more upset that I had to drive to Elmdale today
jokes.setup: David thinks Stevie was upset about engagement
jokes.punchline: Her typical deadpan prioritization of minor inconveniences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie line. Excellent misdirection and character integration. Subverts emotional expectation with mundane concern.

jokes.text: I also appreciate this burlesque-y get up
jokes.setup: Commenting on Moira's cabaret costume
jokes.punchline: Dry appreciation for Moira's theatrical outfit
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Stevie's dry humor about Moira's dramatic presentation.

jokes.text: I also appreciate this burlesque-y get up.
jokes.setup: Looking at Moira's cabaret costume
jokes.punchline: Stevie's dry appreciation of the outfit
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor. Stevie's dry delivery works but it's not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: You're an almost married man, now
jokes.setup: David appreciating Stevie's costume
jokes.punchline: Stevie's mock-serious reminder about his new status
jokes.explanation: Good friend dynamic. Stevie's playful teasing about David's new relationship status.

jokes.text: I'd ask if you could hold us up more, but I'm not a big fan of rhetorical questions.
jokes.setup: Stevie delaying the show further
jokes.punchline: Moira's elaborate way of expressing frustration
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira line - overly intellectual way to express simple frustration. Perfect character voice and very quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, does your Dad not know?
jokes.setup: Johnny doesn't know about engagement
jokes.punchline: Patrick's realization that the news isn't as widespread as it seems
jokes.explanation: Situational comedy about information spreading unevenly. Patrick's genuine confusion works well.

jokes.text: you're very, very cool. And whether you set sail, or stay put, that's not going to change.
jokes.setup: Moira giving Stevie a genuine pep talk
jokes.punchline: Moira's surprisingly insightful and kind assessment
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly sweet moment from Moira. Her genuine appreciation of Stevie's authenticity is touching and well-earned.

jokes.text: Well, you dazzled tonight, sweetheart. Thank you. / Oh, sorry, I don't know why I thought you were talking to me
jokes.setup: Johnny gives praise after the show
jokes.punchline: David's assumption it's for him, then embarrassment when it's not
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing David's self-centeredness and subsequent embarrassment. Well-timed misdirection.

jokes.text: I was shocked! / Shocked? / Amazed. I was amazed
jokes.setup: Johnny accidentally reveals low expectations
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt to cover the insult
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Johnny's accidental honesty. Quick recovery attempt adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Galapa-go now
jokes.setup: Time for Ted to leave for Galapagos
jokes.punchline: Alexis's forced pun mixing goodbye with destination
jokes.explanation: Forced pun that fits Alexis's character tendency toward awkward humor in emotional moments.

jokes.text: it's time for Ted to... Galapa-go now.
jokes.setup: Announcing Ted needs to leave for his trip
jokes.punchline: Forced pun combining Galapagos with 'gotta go'
jokes.explanation: Forced pun that works because it's so deliberately bad. Alexis would absolutely make this kind of pun and be proud of it.

jokes.text: Jocelyn, I'm the one on the chair!
jokes.setup: David tries to make formal announcement
jokes.punchline: Frustrated assertion of his right to announce his own engagement
jokes.explanation: Good frustration comedy. David's need for control over his own announcement being thwarted.

jokes.text: Jocelyn, I'm the one on the chair!
jokes.setup: Jocelyn announcing David's engagement before he can
jokes.punchline: David's frustrated assertion that he should be making the announcement
jokes.explanation: Good callback to David's elaborate announcement fantasy. His frustration at losing control of his moment is very in character.

jokes.text: Oh my God, just spit it out, David!
jokes.setup: David's repeatedly interrupted announcement attempts
jokes.punchline: Alexis's exasperated demand despite already knowing the news
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic. Alexis's impatience despite being part of the problem.

jokes.text: Moira: No! Nooooo!
jokes.setup: Happy engagement celebration
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic wail about the Crows movie being cancelled
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and character work. Moira's dramatic despair cutting through the celebration is peak Schitt's Creek tonal whiplash.

jokes.text: Unless we still wanna do a toast?
jokes.setup: Party ending after Moira's devastating news
jokes.punchline: David's desperate attempt to salvage his celebration
jokes.explanation: Good awkward comedy showing David's inability to read the room. Pathetic attempt to maintain celebration.
